user.title: Equipment
user.description: Select your thermostat and one or more weather sources. The first weather entity is primary; additional ones serve as fallbacks if the primary is unavailable.

thermal_profile.title: Thermal Model
thermal_profile.description: Choose how to initialize the optimizer's thermal model of your home. Learning mode works for everyone; a Beestat profile speeds things up.

data_description.initialization_mode: "Learn automatically" starts fresh and takes 2-3 weeks to fully calibrate using conservative settings. "Import Beestat profile" uses your measured data for faster startup (~3-5 days). "Restore exported model" loads a previously saved model.

thermal_profile_beestat.title: Beestat Profile Import
thermal_profile_beestat.description: Provide the path to your Beestat Temperature Profile JSON file.

data_description.profile_path: Full path inside your Home Assistant instance — no quotes needed around spaces. For HAOS/Docker: upload the file to your config directory (e.g. via Samba or File Editor add-on) and enter /config/beestat_profile.json. For Core installs, use the absolute filesystem path.

thermal_profile_import.title: Restore Exported Model
thermal_profile_import.description: Paste the JSON data from a previously exported model.

data_description.model_import_data: Paste the JSON from the export_model service.

comfort.title: Temperature Boundaries
comfort.description:
The optimizer saves energy by shifting when your HVAC runs. During cheap/efficient hours it pre-cools or pre-heats toward one end of your range; during expensive/inefficient hours it coasts toward the other end.

A wider optimization range means more savings. Safety limits are absolute guardrails that are never exceeded.

data_description.safety_heat_min: Your home will never be cooler than this, even when away or during demand response.
data_description.safety_cool_max: Your home will never be warmer than this, even when away or during demand response.
data_description.comfort_cool_min: During efficient hours, the optimizer pre-cools toward this temperature.
data_description.comfort_cool_max: During inefficient hours, the optimizer lets your home coast up to this temperature.
data_description.comfort_heat_min: During inefficient hours, the optimizer lets your home coast down to this temperature.
data_description.comfort_heat_max: During efficient hours, the optimizer pre-heats toward this temperature.

error.profile_not_found: File not found — check the path is accessible inside your HA container (no quotes needed around spaces)
error.profile_parse_error: Could not parse file as JSON — verify the file is a valid Beestat temperature profile export
error.profile_missing_keys: Missing required data — the file needs temperature deltas (cool_1, heat_1, resist) with linear trendlines and balance points
error.invalid_model_data: Invalid model data — paste the JSON from the export_model service

sensors.title: Environmental & Indoor Sensors
sensors.description: Optional sensors that improve prediction accuracy. Detected sensors are pre-filled — adjust as needed. The optimizer works without these but gets smarter with more data.

energy.title: Energy & Cost Tracking
energy.description: Configure power monitoring, electricity rates, and carbon intensity to enable savings tracking and cost-aware optimization. Detected entities are pre-filled where possible.

behavior.title: Optimizer Behavior
behavior.description: Tune how aggressively the optimizer shifts your HVAC runtime and how it handles manual overrides.

data_description.optimization_aggressiveness: Conservative uses the inner 60% of your comfort range. Balanced uses the full range. Aggressive reoptimizes more often and allows larger corrections.
data_description.override_grace_period_hours: How long to pause optimization after you manually adjust the thermostat.
data_description.reoptimize_interval_hours: How often to rebuild the schedule from the latest forecast.
data_description.max_setpoint_change_per_hour: Limits how quickly the optimizer can change your setpoint to avoid jarring temperature swings.
data_description.use_adaptive_model: Enables online learning of your home's thermal characteristics via Extended Kalman Filter.
data_description.use_greybox_model: Uses a physics-based LP optimizer with learned parameters. Requires the adaptive model.

comfort.title: Temperature Boundaries
comfort.description: Adjust safety limits and the optimization range. A wider optimization range means more savings opportunity.

data_description.safety_heat_min: Your home will never be cooler than this, even when away or during demand response.
data_description.safety_cool_max: Your home will never be warmer than this, even when away or during demand response.
data_description.comfort_cool_min: During efficient hours, the optimizer pre-cools toward this temperature.
data_description.comfort_cool_max: During inefficient hours, the optimizer lets your home coast up to this temperature.
data_description.comfort_heat_min: During inefficient hours, the optimizer lets your home coast down to this temperature.
data_description.comfort_heat_max: During efficient hours, the optimizer pre-heats toward this temperature.

occupancy.title: Occupancy & Presence
occupancy.description: Configure presence detection to widen the optimization range when you're away.

data_description.occupancy_entities: Person entities, binary sensors, or device trackers used to detect if someone is home.
data_description.occupancy_debounce_minutes: How long to wait after the last sensor reports 'away' before switching to away mode.
data_description.away_comfort_delta: How much wider to make the optimization range when everyone is away.

schedule.title: Calendar Scheduling & Pre-Conditioning
schedule.description: Use a calendar entity to plan HVAC optimization around your schedule. The optimizer applies wider comfort ranges when you're away and pre-conditions your home efficiently before you return.

data_description.calendar_entity: A calendar entity whose events indicate your location (e.g., 'Remote', 'In-Person').
data_description.calendar_home_keywords: Comma-separated keywords in event titles that mean you're home (e.g., 'WFH, Remote, Home').
data_description.calendar_away_keywords: Comma-separated keywords in event titles that mean you're away (e.g., 'Office, In-Person, On-Site').
data_description.calendar_default_mode: Occupancy mode to assume when there is no calendar event.
data_description.preconditioning_buffer_minutes: How many minutes before your expected arrival to have the house at the home comfort range.
data_description.departure_zone: Optional zone entity for your workplace. When you leave this zone near the end of an away event, the system refines your arrival estimate.
data_description.travel_time_sensor: Optional sensor providing travel time in minutes from the departure zone to home (e.g., Waze or Google Maps).
data_description.departure_trigger_window_minutes: How many minutes before the calendar event ends to start watching for zone departure.

rooms.title: Room-Aware Sensing
rooms.description: Weight indoor temperature and humidity by room occupancy. When configured, the optimizer uses sensors from rooms you're actually in rather than a flat whole-house average. This reduces manual overrides and improves comfort accuracy.

data_description.indoor_weighting_mode: Equal: all sensors averaged equally (default). Occupied only: only rooms with detected motion contribute. Weighted: occupied rooms count more but unoccupied rooms still contribute.
data_description.room_occupancy_debounce_minutes: How long a room stays 'occupied' after the last motion is detected. Longer values prevent flickering from hallway sensors.
data_description.occupied_weight_multiplier: How much more occupied rooms count vs unoccupied. Only used in Weighted mode.

rooms_discover.title: Select Rooms
rooms_discover.description: These rooms were discovered from your Home Assistant area registry. Select the rooms to include in occupancy-weighted sensing.

error.sensor_overlap: A sensor cannot be used as both indoor and outdoor — please remove the duplicate

force_reoptimize.name: Force Re-optimization
force_reoptimize.description: Immediately re-run the schedule optimizer with the latest forecast.

pause.name: Pause Optimization
pause.description: Pause active optimization and stop writing setpoints to the thermostat.

resume.name: Resume Optimization
resume.description: Resume optimization after a manual pause.

set_occupancy.name: Set Occupancy Mode
set_occupancy.description: Override the occupancy detection with a specific mode.

mode.name: Mode
mode.description: The occupancy mode to set (home, away, vacation, or auto).

demand_response.name: Demand Response
demand_response.description: Activate or deactivate demand response mode to reduce HVAC load.

mode.name: Mode
mode.description: Whether to reduce load or restore normal operation.

duration_minutes.name: Duration (minutes)
duration_minutes.description: How long to maintain demand response mode before auto-restoring.

export_model.name: Export Learned Model
export_model.description: Export the current learned thermal model parameters as JSON.

import_model.name: Import Learned Model
import_model.description: Import previously exported thermal model parameters.

model_data.name: Model Data
model_data.description: The model data dictionary previously returned by export_model.

set_constraint.name: Set Constraint
set_constraint.description: Apply a temporary constraint from an external energy manager or automation.

type.name: Constraint type
type.description: The type of constraint: max_temp, min_temp, max_power, or pause_until.

value.name: Value
value.description: The constraint value (temperature in °F, power in watts, or ignored for pause_until).

duration_minutes.name: Duration (minutes)
duration_minutes.description: How long the constraint remains active before auto-expiring.

source.name: Source
source.description: Identifier for the integration or automation that set this constraint.
